name: Castillo de La Adrada, puente Mocha y Charca de los Cangrejos — circular desde La Adrada
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around La Adrada are set within the Tiétar Valley, bordered by the Sierra de Gredos. The region features a diverse natural environment with lush plains, narrow gorges, and crystal-clear water pools. Cyclists can explore dense pine, holm oak, and chestnut forests, as well as routes alongside the Tiétar River. The terrain includes forest tracks, mountain trails, and secondary roads, providing varied experiences. Best touring cycling routes around La Adrada The most popular touring cycling route is Castillo de La Adrada, puente Mocha y Charca de los Cangrejos — circular desde La Adrada, a 12.2 miles (19.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete, combining historical sites with natural pools. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Puente Mosquea – La Adrada Castle loop from La Adrada, a moderate 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. This route features historical Roman bridges and views of La Adrada Castle. Local touring cyclists also love the Cuesta Blanca loop from Piedralaves, a 15.3 miles (24.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around La Adrada is defined by pine forests, the Tiétar River, and historical Roman bridges. The network offers options for various ability levels, from accessible paths to more challenging trails. The routes in La Adrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore La Adrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around La Adrada?
A: There are over 18 touring cycling routes around La Adrada, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include 3 easy, 6 moderate, and 9 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in La Adrada?
A: The routes offer diverse terrain, from lush plains and dense forests of pine, holm oak, and chestnut to narrow gorges and secondary roads. You'll find forest tracks, mountain trails, and paths alongside the Tiétar River, all set against the backdrop of the Tiétar Valley and Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in La Adrada?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105145235/castillo-de-la-adrada-puente-mocha-y-charca-de-los-cangrejos-circular-desde-la-adrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de La Adrada, puente Mocha y Charca de los Cangrejos — circular desde La Adrada&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that combines historical sites with natural pools.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant landmarks. You can explore the historic castle of La Adrada, which houses the Historical Interpretation Center of the Tiétar Valley. Natural attractions include crystal-clear natural pools like El Charco de la Hoya and Charca de la Nieta, and the scenic Tiétar River. Some routes also feature historical Roman bridges. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10372135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mosquea – La Adrada Castle loop from La Adrada&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the castle and Roman bridges.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in La Adrada?
A: Yes, La Adrada offers routes suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not detailed, the region's diverse network includes easier paths through pine forests and along rivers that can be enjoyed by cyclists of varying abilities. It's advisable to check the difficulty grade of individual routes on komoot to find the best fit for your family.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in La Adrada?
A: The region experiences diverse seasonal conditions. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended to avoid the peak heat. Winters are milder but can have cooler temperatures.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in La Adrada?
A: La Adrada is a well-known destination for cyclists, and parking is generally available within the town and at common starting points for routes. Specific parking information for individual routes can often be found in the route descriptions on komoot.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in La Adrada?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse natural environment, including the dense forests and crystal-clear water pools, as well as the combination of natural beauty with historical elements like Roman bridges and La Adrada Castle.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant elevation changes for a challenge?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes with substantial elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuesta Blanca loop from Piedralaves&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.3-mile (24.7 km) trail with significant climbing, often completed in about 2 hours and 9 minutes.

Q: Can I rent bikes in La Adrada?
A: Yes, bike rental services, including e-bikes, are available in La Adrada. This allows visitors to explore the region's extensive network of routes even if they don't bring their own equipment.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for multi-hour rides?
A: Absolutely. For longer excursions, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31882758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toros de Guisando – La Adrada Castle loop from La Adrada&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 31 miles (50 km) and can take around 3 hours and 10 minutes, offering a more extensive exploration of the area's landscapes and landmarks.

Q: What natural monuments or lakes can be found near the cycling routes?
A: Near La Adrada, you can find several natural monuments and lakes. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Casillas – Entrance to the Valle de Iruelas Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Abuelo Chestnut Tree&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4327965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca de los Cangrejos&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent feature in the area, offering scenic views.
tips:
Built during the 14th-15th century and declared an Asset of Cultural Interest
May 5, 1949 is a totally recommendable visit. It is very well preserved and inside you can watch an explanatory video about its history. It is worth going up to the castle towers from where there are spectacular views. The entrance price is 3 euros.
Next to the road there is a bridge of Roman origin, it is the Mocha bridge that allows us to cross the Tiétar river surrounded by a beautiful environment.
Pond at the edge of the road with very easy access that in the wet seasons creates a very beautiful landscape.
